name: openwifi
summary: openwifi: Linux mac80211 compatible full-stack IEEE802.11/Wi-Fi design based on SDR (Software Defined Radio).

documentation.readme: openwifi: Linux mac80211 compatible full-stack IEEE802.11/Wi-Fi design based on SDR (Software Defined Radio).
usage_sections: We remain committed to open source, which is our foundation. To access advanced features and dedicated support, consider a SUBSCRIPTION. More info on https://openwifi.tech
20MHz bandwidth; 70 MHz to 6 GHz frequency range
Mode tested: Ad-hoc; Station; AP, Monitor
DCF (CSMA/CA) low MAC layer in FPGA (10us SIFS is achieved)

arguments.name: --target
arguments.description: Target host, IP, MAC, or URL the tool operates on.

arguments.name: --interface
arguments.description: Network interface to bind to (e.g. wlan0mon, eth0).

arguments.name: --output
arguments.description: Output file path for the report or capture.

arguments.name: --verbose
arguments.description: Increase log verbosity (repeatable for higher levels).

arguments.name: --wordlist
arguments.description: Path to a wordlist (users, passwords, paths).

arguments.name: --proxy
arguments.description: HTTP or SOCKS proxy URL for outbound requests.

arguments.name: --header
arguments.description: Extra HTTP header in key:value form (repeatable).

arguments.name: --insecure
arguments.description: Skip TLS certificate verification (lab use only).

arguments.name: --json
arguments.description: Emit JSON output instead of human-readable text.

arguments.name: --dry-run
arguments.description: Print what would be done without making any changes.

function_signatures.name: configure
function_signatures.description: Configure the tool with runtime options (interface, target, etc.) before execution.

function_signatures.name: scan
function_signatures.description: Perform a read-only scan / probe of the target.

function_signatures.name: exploit
function_signatures.description: Run the offensive step against the target. Returns ok, data, error envelope.

function_signatures.name: report
function_signatures.description: Render the result into a human-readable or machine-readable report.

function_signatures.name: validate
function_signatures.description: Pre-flight check: confirm the target is reachable and the tool can run.

function_signatures.name: cleanup
function_signatures.description: Remove any artifacts left behind by the tool (pcaps, sessions, files).

function_signatures.name: chain
function_signatures.description: Take a previous tool's result and feed it as input to this tool.

examples: sudo dd bs=512 count=31116288 if=openwifi-xyz.img of=/dev/your_sdcard_dev
raspi-config --expand-rootfs (Only needed when your SD card > 16GB. Run and reboot)
./openwifi/setup_once.sh (Reboot the board. Only need to run once for new board)
sudo iptables -t nat -A POSTROUTING -o NICY -j MASQUERADE
sudo ip route add $KFIOSA_TARGET_HOST/24 via $KFIOSA_TARGET_HOST dev ethX
documentation.how_to_use: Run the primary entrypoint as shown in upstream docs, e.g. `sudo dd bs=512 count=31116288 if=openwifi-xyz.img of=/dev/your_sdcard_dev`. Further invocations from upstream: `ssh root@$KFIOSA_TARGET_HOST`; `raspi-config --expand-rootfs (Only needed when your SD card > 16GB. Run and reboot)`. openwifi: Linux mac80211 compatible full-stack IEEE802.11/Wi-Fi design based on SDR (Software Defined Radio).
documentation.when_to_use: Use during the 'any' phase against surfaces [wifi_2_4_ghz, wifi_5_ghz, shell_linux] in the Wireless category. Fits when the engagement needs: openwifi: Linux mac80211 compatible full-stack IEEE802.11/Wi-Fi design based on SDR (Software Defined Radio).
documentation.why_to_use: Prefer this tool based on upstream description: openwifi: Linux mac80211 compatible full-stack IEEE802.11/Wi-Fi design based on SDR (Software Defined Radio).
